generated_from.unit_screenshots: UnitsScreenshots/ user-provided in-game info screenshots, OCR-normalized 2026-05-02

purpose: Machine-readable units and terrains dictionary for a UniWar engine, bot, and training pipeline.
null_policy: null means unknown, unpublished, unresolved, or intentionally not guessed from incomplete public sources.

confidence_levels.official: Current official unit cards or official public rules pages.
confidence_levels.mixed: Combined from official report content and stable secondary sources.
confidence_levels.community_consensus: Stable community-derived behavior noted in the research report, but not fully current official publication.
confidence_levels.unpublished: The report explicitly says the public web does not publish the numeric value.

notes: The report says ground light units benefit most from base, forest, and mountain terrain.
The report says ground heavy units prefer open/plain/desert terrain and do not enter mountains.
This comes from older community summaries and remains directionally useful for pure aquatic units.
The report notes that amphibian units are modern official additions and old public how-to-play pages are outdated.
Amphibian is an official modern unit type, but the public tutorial pages in the report do not fully publish its terrain matrix.
Baseline neutral land terrain.

source_notes: Surface-mode submerged target strength is the explicit Submerged attack row from current in-game unit screenshots, not a derived aquatic-minus-penalty value.
Surface-mode values are derived from the official Aquatic attack stat plus the official Attack Underwater units penalty when that penalty is published.
Hidden-mode values are derived from the official Aquatic attack stat plus the official Attack from Underwater penalty when that penalty is published.
submerged_target_attack.surface_mode_source: UnitsScreenshots OCR explicit Submerged attack row, 2026-05-02
submerged_target_attack.surface_same_hex_source: Current in-game screenshots: 0 in attack range means same-hex attack against a submerged hidden target, 2026-05-02

Hidden-mode values are derived from the official Aquatic attack stat plus the official Attack from Underwater penalty when that penalty is published.
submerged_target_attack.surface_mode_source: UnitsScreenshots OCR explicit Submerged attack row, 2026-05-02
submerged_target_attack.surface_same_hex_source: Current in-game screenshots: 0 in attack range means same-hex attack against a submerged hidden target, 2026-05-02

mantisse.terrain_effects_source: OCR-normalized from UnitsScreenshots in-game info tables; values are per terrain row shown by the current client.

hidden_mode.terrain_movement_costs_source: Uses the displayed submerged aquatic terrain table with the submerged mobility pool.
hidden_mode.can_attack_ground_air_from_hidden_source: UnitsScreenshots OCR Attack Ground/Air Units From Underwater line, 2026-05-02

source_notes: Surface-mode submerged target strength is the explicit Submerged attack row from current in-game unit screenshots, not a derived aquatic-minus-penalty value.
Surface-mode values are derived from the official Aquatic attack stat plus the official Attack Underwater units penalty when that penalty is published.
Hidden-mode values are derived from the official Aquatic attack stat plus the official Attack from Underwater penalty when that penalty is published.
submerged_target_attack.surface_mode_source: UnitsScreenshots OCR explicit Submerged attack row, 2026-05-02
submerged_target_attack.surface_same_hex_source: Current in-game screenshots: 0 in attack range means same-hex attack against a submerged hidden target, 2026-05-02

skimmer.terrain_effects_source: OCR-normalized from UnitsScreenshots in-game info tables; values are per terrain row shown by the current client.

known_gaps.description: Movement costs and displayed attack/defense modifiers are now populated from user-provided in-game unit info screenshots; remaining validation is exact naming of newer terrain variants such as road_water, road_land, ocean, reef, city, and chasm against server map serialization.
The exact recharge after teleport for Mecha, Mecha II, Guardian, and Eclipse is not published in the current public official pages.
The report says the exact public rules for detecting submerged units are incomplete.
Support repair multipliers are known, but the exact stacking rule for multiple support sources is not fully published.
Veterancy bonuses are known, but the XP gain formula is community consensus rather than fully current official publication.

submerged_target_attack_strength.surface_source: UnitsScreenshots explicit Submerged attack row per unit.
submerged_target_attack_strength.can_attack_flag_semantics: surface_mode_can_attack and hidden_mode_can_attack are authoritative legality flags; zero strength can still be legal for selected units when the flag is true and terrain/gang-up bonuses apply.
submerged_target_attack_strength.null_meaning: No screenshot-backed value or the unit cannot attack submerged targets in that mode.
notes: Surface values were reconciled against user-provided current in-game screenshots on 2026-05-02.
Bopper, Borfly, and Guardian have explicit 0 submerged strength but can attack submerged units when bonuses raise effective attack.
Support units with all-zero direct attacks remain unable to attack even though the UI row displays 0.
Submarine can attack ground/air units while underwater; Kraken and Skimmer cannot, per the screenshot line Attack Ground/Air Units From Underwater.

terrain_effect_matrix.source: UnitsScreenshots/*.jpg, OCR-normalized from the current in-game unit info terrain tables.
terrain_effect_matrix.blocked_semantics: A null mobility_cost means the terrain row is not available for that unit class in the screenshot table and is treated as impassable by movement generation.
